title: Lepidolopsis scopulorum
intro: Lepidolopsis scopulorum, commonly known as the Himalayan lepidolopsis or Indian cobra plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antivenom properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepidolopsis scopulorum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of a paste or powder in various herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Lepidolopsis scopulorum is a species of plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants, with Lepidolopsis being the only recognized genus in the tribe Gomphogyneae. Lepidolopsis scopulorum is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in China and Myanmar. The classification of Lepidolopsis scopulorum is currently recognized under the family Cucurbitaceae. Further classification details may be found within the tribe Gomphogyneae.
